summary.scholar: The Statue of Liberty was dedicated on October 28, 1886 in New York Harbor. A gift from France designed by Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi, the 151-foot copper statue became the most iconic symbol of American freedom and immigration. For millions of immigrants arriving by ship, the statue was their first sight of America.
summary.explorer: On October 28, 1886, the Statue of Liberty was unveiled in New York Harbor! France gave it to America as a gift of friendship. The giant copper lady holding a torch became the ultimate symbol of freedom. For millions of people coming to America from other countries, she was the first thing they saw!

significance.scholar: The Statue of Liberty became the preeminent symbol of American democracy and immigration, representing the nation's ideals of freedom and opportunity to the world.
significance.explorer: Lady Liberty has been welcoming people to America for over 130 years. She stands for freedom and the idea that everyone deserves a chance at a better life.

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
